name: 45-Degree Back Extension
resistance_profile: Trains the erectors (and hips) through a loaded hinge; add load held at the chest.
rom_notes: Hinge to a full stretch, extend to a neutral (not hyperextended) torso.
cues: Extend to neutral — don't crank into hyperextension
execution_steps: Set the pad just below your hip crease and lock your feet under the ankle pads.
Cross your arms on your chest (or hold a plate against it) with your torso straight, in line with your legs.
Hinge at the hips and lower your torso down until you feel a stretch through the hamstrings and lower back.
Squeeze your glutes and erectors to raise your torso back up to neutral — in line with your legs, not arched past it.
Pause briefly at the top, then control the descent into the next rep.
good_when: You want to train the erectors and hips with almost no spinal compression (no bar on your back).
You're a beginner building lower-back strength and hinge endurance.
You need low-fatigue posterior-chain accessory work after heavy deadlifts or squats.
bad_when: You want to load the pattern heavily for maximal strength — an RDL or deadlift scales far better.
No 45-degree bench or GHD is available.
